item.title: Education
item.description:
🎓 Cornell University

M.Eng. in Data Science & Decision Analytics · New York, NY · May 2026

Honors & Recognition:

  • Merit Scholarship recipient
  • GPA: 3.93

Relevant Coursework:

  • Applied Machine Learning
  • Applied Data Science
  • Optimization Methods
  • Revenue Management

Key Achievements:

  • Engineered deep learning pipeline with 73.9% F1 score on 87K+ ECG classifications
  • Specialized in data science, machine learning, optimization, and research methodologies
  • Conducting research at the Interaction Research Lab

Location: Cornell Tech, New York, NY

item.title: Experience
item.description:
Professional Journey

I’ve worked on impactful projects spanning research, data analysis, and machine learning. Here’s how I’ve grown as a data scientist.

🤖 Interaction Research Lab, Cornell Tech

Independent Researcher · New York, NY · December 2025 – Present

Collaborating on human-AI interaction research for real-world deployment in retail environments.

What I’m doing:

  • Architecting data models and storage strategies for multimodal data (interaction logs, sensor data, video)
  • Designing longitudinal study metrics to evaluate robot social capabilities
  • Conducting exploratory analyses of interaction patterns and social behaviors

Skills applied: Data engineering, Research design, Database architecture, Data exploration

item.title: EGC Heartbeat Classification and Deep Learning Analysis
item.description:
🧠 ECG Heartbeat Classification with Transfer Learning

Duration: September – December 2024
Dataset: 87,000+ ECG heartbeat images
Target: 5 diagnostic categories (Normal, PVC, PAC, Fusion, Unknown)
Performance: F1 Score 0.739 (+40% vs. baseline CNN)

The Clinical Problem

Cardiac arrhythmias affect millions globally, yet manual ECG screening is time-consuming and prone to human error. Healthcare systems need automated classification systems that are:

  • Accurate: Minimize false negatives (missed abnormalities)
  • Fast: Real-time processing at scale
  • Reliable: Handle class imbalance and noisy data

Challenge: ECG datasets suffer from severe class imbalance (abnormal cases are rare) and domain shift between hospitals.

item.title: DataFest — CourseKata Engagement Analysis
item.description:
🏆 DataFest Competition — Best Insight Award Winner

Duration: January – May 2024
Award: Winner of the Best Insight Award
Scale: 1.5 GB of student engagement data

The Challenge

CourseKata, an educational platform for teaching data science and statistics, wanted to understand what drives meaningful engagement with their textbooks. Our team competed in DataFest to uncover insights that would help them improve learning outcomes for thousands of students.

Research question: What drives engagement and retention during learning?
